attributes.description:
The delimiter to use when setting the value when `multiple` is enabled.
The default is a space, but you can set it to a comma or other character.
attributes.name: delimiter

attributes.description: The name of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the select is empty.
attributes.name: placeholder

attributes.description: Allows more than one option to be selected.
attributes.name: multiple

attributes.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.
attributes.name: max-options-visible

attributes.description: Sets the select to a readonly state.
attributes.name: readonly

attributes.description: Adds a clear button when the select is not empty.
attributes.name: clearable

attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the select is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the menu, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the select's open state.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description: The select's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.
attributes.name: label

attributes.description:
The preferred placement of the select's menu. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the listbox
inside of the viewport.
attributes.name: placement

attributes.description: The select's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
attributes.name: help-text

attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
attributes.name: form

attributes.description:
A function that customizes the tags to be rendered when multiple=true. The first argument is the option, the second
is the current tag's index.  The function should return either a Lit TemplateResult or a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at
the specified value.
attributes.name: getTag
attributes.type: (option: SynOption, index: number) => TemplateResult | string | HTMLElement

attributes.description:
The current value of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data. When `multiple` is enabled, the
value attribute will be a space-delimited list of values based on the options selected, and the value property will
be an array. **For this reason, values must not contain spaces.**
attributes.name: value

cssParts.description: The form control that wraps the label, input, and help text.
cssParts.name: form-control

cssParts.description: The container the wraps the prefix, suffix, combobox, clear icon, and expand button.
cssParts.name: combobox

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the prefix slot.
cssParts.name: prefix

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the suffix slot.
cssParts.name: suffix

cssParts.description: The element that displays the selected option's label, an `<input>` element.
cssParts.name: display-input

cssParts.description: The listbox container where options are slotted.
cssParts.name: listbox

cssParts.description: The container that houses option tags when `multiselect` is used.
cssParts.name: tags

cssParts.description: The individual tags that represent each multiselect option.
cssParts.name: tag

cssParts.description: The tag's remove button base part.
cssParts.name: tag__remove-button__base

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the expand icon.
cssParts.name: expand-icon

cssParts.description: The popup's exported `popup` part. Use this to target the tooltip's popup container.
cssParts.name: popup

events.description: Emitted when the control's value changes.
events.name: syn-change

events.description: Emitted when the control's value is cleared.
events.name: syn-clear

events.description: Emitted when the control receives input.
events.name: syn-input

events.description: Emitted when the control gains focus.
events.name: syn-focus

events.description: Emitted when the control loses focus.
events.name: syn-blur

events.description: Emitted when the select's menu opens.
events.name: syn-show

events.description: Emitted after the select's menu opens and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-show

events.description: Emitted when the select's menu closes.
events.name: syn-hide

events.description: Emitted after the select's menu closes and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-hide

events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.
events.name: syn-invalid

methods.description: Checks for validity but does not show a validation message. Returns `true` when valid and `false` when invalid.
methods.name: checkValidity

methods.description: Gets the associated form, if one exists.
methods.name: getForm

methods.description: Checks for validity and shows the browser's validation message if the control is invalid.
methods.name: reportValidity

methods.description: Sets a custom validation message. Pass an empty string to restore validity.
methods.name: setCustomValidity

properties.description:
The delimiter to use when setting the value when `multiple` is enabled.
The default is a space, but you can set it to a comma or other character.
properties.name: delimiter

properties.description: The name of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: name

properties.description:
The current value of the select, submitted as a name/value pair with form data. When `multiple` is enabled, the
value attribute will be a space-delimited list of values based on the options selected, and the value property will
be an array. **For this reason, values must not contain spaces.**
properties.name: value

properties.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.
properties.name: defaultValue

properties.description: The select's size.
properties.name: size

properties.description: Placeholder text to show as a hint when the select is empty.
properties.name: placeholder

properties.description: Allows more than one option to be selected.
properties.name: multiple

properties.description:
The maximum number of selected options to show when `multiple` is true. After the maximum, "+n" will be shown to
indicate the number of additional items that are selected. Set to 0 to remove the limit.
properties.name: maxOptionsVisible

properties.description: Disables the select control.
properties.name: disabled

properties.description: Sets the select to a readonly state.
properties.name: readonly

properties.description: Adds a clear button when the select is not empty.
properties.name: clearable

properties.description:
Indicates whether or not the select is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the menu, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the select's open state.
properties.name: open

properties.description: The select's label. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.
properties.name: label

properties.description:
The preferred placement of the select's menu. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the listbox
inside of the viewport.
properties.name: placement

properties.description: The select's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
properties.name: helpText

properties.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
properties.name: form

properties.description: The select's required attribute.
properties.name: required

properties.description:
A function that customizes the tags to be rendered when multiple=true. The first argument is the option, the second
is the current tag's index.  The function should return either a Lit TemplateResult or a string containing trusted HTML of the symbol to render at
the specified value.
properties.name: getTag
properties.type: (option: SynOption, index: number) => TemplateResult | string | HTMLElement

properties.description: Gets the validity state object
properties.name: validity

properties.description: Gets the validation message
properties.name: validationMessage

slots.description: The listbox options. Must be `<syn-option>` elements. You can use `<syn-divider>` to group items visually.
The input's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.
slots.name: label

slots.description: Used to prepend a presentational icon or similar element to the combobox.
slots.name: prefix

slots.description: Used to append a presentational icon or similar element to the combobox.
slots.name: suffix

slots.description: An icon to use in lieu of the default clear icon.
slots.name: clear-icon

slots.description: The icon to show when the control is expanded and collapsed. Rotates on open and close.
slots.name: expand-icon

slots.description: Text that describes how to use the input. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.
slots.name: help-text

summary: Selects allow you to choose items from a menu of predefined options.
